column17: Chart Designer: Dr. Thomas R. Nicely

column2: With a 9-7 record the 1983 Lions rose to the top of a weak NFC Central, to claim their first division championship since 1957. In the playoffs the Lions led the San Francisco 49ers late in the 4th Quarter, until Joe Montana drove the 49ers down the field for a 14-yard touchdown pass to Freddie Solomon to give the 49ers a 24-23 lead. The Lions would havehad  a chance to win the game, when Gary Danielson drove them into field goal range, but Kicker Eddie Murray missed a 44-yard field goal with five seconds remaining.
